title: eryngium divaricatum
intro: Eryngium divaricatum, also known as the forked sea holly, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections, making it a potential treatment for various ailments. Eryngium divaricatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
